messages.content: Use concrete technical reasoning at all times.

how do I use the esp32 matter development framework to make a shade? I already have a lot of the code to make this work, I want to focus on the root node binding and further develop this specific part of the firmware. attached is the current code i'm working with. I want to be able to bind to a binary state attribute from a contact sensor. the goal here is to design a cover controller that doesn't need to use a stateful motor because it will allow binding to another contact sensor on the fabric. 

I have a binding that works but i dont really know how to get messages from the client after I subscribe. I want to show you my app code and get your help strengthening the logic around the binding client such that I have robust event handling when the bound contact sensor changes.

i am also interested in figuring out how to set constraints on bindings. how would I reject a binding entry if I find the user submitting a binding entry I want my embedded application to reject?

lastly, I want to make sure that the clients are properly deallocated and closed when binding entries are removed from the binding table.

messages.thinking:
The user wants help with binding client logic, subscribing to attribute changes, handling events robustly, rejecting binding entries with constraints, and cleaning up clients when binding entries removed. Need to refer to esp_matter client API, binding manager, cluster_update, request_callback, subscription handling using ReadClient, etc. Provide concrete code snippets, steps, guidelines. Also mention how to use binding table callbacks (kBindingsChangedViaCluster) and custom validation using binding table modification callbacks (maybe via binding_manager_init and custom logic). Show example of request_callback that subscribes to BooleanState::StateValue attribute on contact sensor endpoint, using interaction::subscribe::send_request with ReadClient::Callback implementation to handle updates. Use binding manager to retrieve binding entries, iterate, connect to each node, subscribe. Provide handling of errors, reconnection, auto resubscribe. Provide way to reject binding: implement attribute update callback for Binding cluster's Bind attribute (or use binding table validation callback via server). Possibly using Matter's binding table implementation: check binding entry before adding, using binding_manager's callback? Might need to intercept on kBindingsChangedViaCluster event and validate, then delete if not allowed.

Also ensure deallocation: keep track of peer_device_t pointers and ReadClient objects, free after unsubscribe or when binding removed. Use esp_matter::client::set_request_callback to set request callback.

Provide sample code modifications in app_main: after creating binding cluster, set request callback, define request_callback that subscribes to attribute and registers read callback. Provide struct to hold subscription state.

Also discuss using AccessControl to check permissions.

Will answer with step-by-step guide, code snippets, explanation.

messages.content:
I want to slightly refactor how this subscription manager works. first, dont worry about mutex isolation, I will handle that myself at a later stage. next, I want to change the signature of the addbinding such that it works like this.

I loop through the binding table and I incrementally call this:

SubscriptionManager::AddBinding(const chip::app::Clusters::Binding::TableEntry &entry, std::map<uint64_t, std::unique_ptr<Subscription>> &new_sub_assemble);

on the stack that this operates on will store a map and that is referenced in the AddBinding. AddBinding either creates a new Subscription or loads the existing one for each time the AddBinding is called, and as it is called, that subscription is added to the new subs map.

then, at the end of looping that binding table, this is called:

SubscriptionManager::FinishAdditions(std::map<uint64_t, std::unique_ptr<Subscription>> &new_sub_assemble);

at this stage, the removed binding entries are determined and handled.

please execute this change exactly as I've described it with perfect memory safety and technical correctness. remember, the simplest solution is the best.

messages.thinking:
We need to modify code to reflect new design. Provide updated header and implementation. Remove mutex, adapt AddBinding signature, add FinishAdditions. Need to manage existing map (current subs). We'll keep existing manager with internal map `m_subs`. AddBinding will take entry and reference to temporary map `new_sub_assemble`. It will check if entry already exists in m_subs; if so move pointer to new map (or copy pointer). If not, create new Subscription, store in new map, also perhaps start connection later? But we need to start subscription immediately? In original design AddBinding creates subscription and starts it. Now we want to defer removal until FinishAdditions, but still need to start subscription for new entries as we add.

Simplify: Keep m_subs as current map. AddBinding will either find existing subscription in m_subs (by key) and move it into new_sub_assemble (by moving unique_ptr). If not found, create new Subscription and start connection (same as before) and put into new_sub_assemble.

FinishAdditions will then iterate over m_subs (old map) and for any entry not present in new_sub_assemble, call RemoveBinding (abort). Then replace m_subs with new_sub_assemble (move).

Implementation details: need helper to compute key from entry (fabric, nodeId, endpoint). Entry type: chip::app::Clusters::Binding::TableEntry (maybe same as chip::Binding::BindingEntry). We'll assume has fields: fabricIndex, remote.nodeId, remote.endpoint, remote.cluster maybe etc. Use same MakeKey.

We'll adjust header: SubscriptionManager with methods AddBinding(entry, new_map) and FinishAdditions(new_map). Remove internal mutex.

We'll also adjust binding_table_changed to use new flow: create temporary map, loop over entries calling AddBinding(entry,temp_map); after loop call FinishAdditions(temp_map). No need to handle removal inside loop.

We'll also adjust existing RemoveBinding to be internal, maybe private.

Let's produce updated files: binding_subscriptions.hpp and .cpp with new signatures, and updated snippet of binding_table_changed.

We'll also note that we removed mutex, but still need thread safety later.

Let's craft final answer with diff-like patches.
